'''Kurt Geoffrey Gidley''' (born 7 June 1982) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> An [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] international and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative and captain, he previously played in the [[National Rugby League]] for the [[Newcastle Knights]], captaining them for 123 games. He played as a {{rlp|fb|fe|hb}} and {{rlp|hk}}, although due to his versatility, he was able to slip into the positions of {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|lk}} when needed throughout his career.

In 2011, Gidley competed in the third season of the [[Seven Network|Channel Seven]] television series ''[[Australia's Greatest Athlete (season 3)|Australia's Greatest Athlete]]''.

==Early and personal life==
Gidley was born in [[Newcastle, New South Wales]], Australia.

He is the younger brother of [[Matthew Gidley]], and is the youngest of three brothers. He was an apprentice butcher when he left high school. Gidley's current partner is Brooke McNamara.<ref name="Ideal cure for Kurt's blu">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/ideal-cure-for-kurts-blues/story-e6freye0-1225881774824|title=Ideal cure for Kurt's blu|publisher=dailytelegraph.com.au|date=20 June 2010|access-date=1 January 2011}}</ref> He was a [[Hunter Mariners]] junior.<ref name="Hunter Mariners at rl1908.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.rl1908.com/Clubs/Hunter-Mariners.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110518112542/http://www.rl1908.com/Clubs/Hunter-Mariners.htm|url-status=dead|archive-date=18 May 2011|title=Hunter Mariners at rl1908.com|publisher=rl1908.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===2000s===
Gidley made his NRL debut for the Newcastle Knights in 2001 at 19 years of age where he played Five-eighth in a round 24 loss to [[St George Illawarra]]. However Gidley did not feature in the Knights successful finals run.

After winning the [[2001 NRL Premiership]], the Knights travelled to England to play the [[2002 World Club Challenge]] against [[Super League]] champions, the [[Bradford Bulls]]. Gidley played on the {{rlp|wg}} in Newcastle's loss. Gidley had a productive 2004 season (being 18th man for the NSW Blues). In round 10 of the [[2004 NRL season]] Gidley made history when he won the Knights' first ever golden point match by kicking the winning field goal after 3 minutes and 26 seconds of extra time. The Knights were without [[Andrew Johns]] for the season. That game was against the Broncos at [[Lang Park]] (Suncorp Stadium). Gidley missed most of the [[2005 NRL season]] due to injury.{{Cn|date=June 2025}}

In 2006, Gidley travelled to [[Papua New Guinea]] to play for the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] against the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Kumuls]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Sports Australia|title=ARL announce Kangaroos squad for Tri-Nations|url=http://sportsaustralia.com/articles/oct06/artid6557.html|access-date=13 October 2008|date=2 October 2006|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080726020526/http://sportsaustralia.com/articles/oct06/artid6557.html|archive-date=26 July 2008|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Gidley had a productive season in 2007. He scored multiple times for the [[Newcastle Knights]] as well as having done well at converting goals. He scored 15 points against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in their round 5 clash including 2 tries 3/4 goals and a field goal. He has also represented NSW in [[2007 State of Origin]]. 
[[File:Kurt Gidley.JPG|thumb|right|Gidley playing for the Knights in 2008]]He was then selected in the [[2008 State of Origin series]] shortly after a fractured cheekbone Gidley was the stand-out player at the club in the 2008 season as they finished ninth on the NRL ladder with 12 wins and 12 losses. Gidley led the scoring at the club with 150 points and also took the try-scoring award with nine tries. Regarded as one of the best utility players in the game, Gidley was also appointed captain of the Knights for the next season, replacing England-bound {{rlp|hk}} and club legend [[Danny Buderus]]. In August 2008, Gidley was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team#Rugby League World Cup Squad|preliminary 46-man Kangaroos squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref>{{cite news|author=Liam FitzGibbon |publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia) |title=Surprises in Kangaroos squad |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24111780-23214,00.html |access-date=2 August 2008 |date=1 August 2008 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080808144730/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0%2C8659%2C24111780-23214%2C00.html |archive-date= 8 August 2008 }}</ref><ref name="Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad">{{cite news|url=http://www.iht.com/articles/ap/2008/08/01/sports/AS-RGL-Australia-Squad.php|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080916064704/http://www.iht.com/articles/ap/2008/08/01/sports/AS-RGL-Australia-Squad.php|url-status=dead|archive-date=16 September 2008|title=Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad|newspaper=International Herald Tribune|date=1 August 2008|access-date=2 August 2008}}</ref> and in October 2008 he was selected in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Australia|Australia]] squad.<ref name="Manly six win Australia call-up">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7656211.stm|title=Manly six win Australia call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref> He was selected for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the one-off test match against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] on 8 May 2009.<ref>{{cite news|work=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]|title=Kangaroos selectors stick by losing World Cup team for New Zealand Test|first=Tom|last=Wald|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422175-5016947,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121204183728/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422175-5016947,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=4 December 2012|date=3 May 2009|access-date=3 May 2009}}</ref>
[[File:Kurt Gidley and Benji Marshall (26 April 2009).jpg|thumb|right|Gidley trying to tackle [[Benji Marshall]] in 2009]]In May 2009, he was named as captain and {{rlp|fb}} to represent New South Wales in the opening State of Origin match on 3 June 2009, in Melbourne.<ref>{{cite news|last=Fitzgibbon|first=Liam|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|title=NSW selectors go with form and not reputation for Origin opener|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25537374-5018993,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120730123123/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25537374-5018993,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=30 July 2012|access-date=30 May 2009|date=25 May 2009}}</ref> In the 2009 season Kurt Gidley again led the club's season in points as the Knights finished 7th after a good year. Kurt also achieved the second best percentage of kicking goals, with 82.1%, only being beaten by [[Michael Gordon (rugby league)|Michael Gordon]] with 93.4%. In November 2009, he was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian national team]] to take part in the [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|2009 Four Nations]]. In Australia's opening game of the tournament against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] he played as the 17th man utility, making an impact in the last quarter of the game. In game three against [[France national rugby league team|France]], he was chosen to replace regular {{rlp|fb}} [[Billy Slater]] who was rested along with others. He successfully converted two goals which played part in the final 42–4 Australian win. On 7 November, Gidley was chosen on the interchange bench for the Four Nations final played in Leeds in which the Kangaroos thrashed England 46–16. He was brought on into the {{rlp|ce}}s to replace an injured [[Justin Hodges]] early in the game.

===2010s===
In January 2010, Gidley was named in the [[NRL All Stars team]] to play on 13 February 2010.<ref>{{cite web|last=Dillon|first=Robert|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/450925/gidley-backs-all-stars-in-passion-play-kurt-confident-intensity-high-in-both-camps/|title=Gidley backs All Stars in passion play: Kurt confident intensity high in both camps | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2010-01-28|access-date=2016-02-06}}</ref> He was voted as the Knight's representative after beating [[Ben Cross (rugby league)|Ben Cross]], [[Chris Houston (rugby league)|Chris Houston]] and [[Jarrod Mullen]]. Kurt suffered a knee injury in the game which left him side-lined until Round 6 of the 2010 season.<ref name="Kurt Gidley and Darren Lockyer undergo scans after All Stars clash">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/kurt-gidley-and-darren-lockyer-undergo-scans-after-all-stars-clash/story-e6frexnr-1225830191848|title=Kurt Gidley and Darren Lockyer undergo scans after All Stars clash|publisher=dailytelegraph.com.au|date= 14 February 2010|access-date=1 January 2011}}</ref> For the [[2010 ANZAC Test]], Gidley was selected to play for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] from the interchange bench in their victory against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. On 7 May, 28 minutes into the Test match, starting Australian {{rlp|hk}} [[Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)|Cameron Smith]] came off the field with a shoulder injury, which led to Gidley coming on and playing [[Rugby league positions#dummy half|dummy half]] for the rest of the game. On 17 May 2010 Gidley was named as {{rlp|fb}} and captain of the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Blues]]. For the first time in Origin history, Gidley captained the side from the reserves bench for Game 2. During a team bonding session on 10 June 2010 Gidley's ex teammate and friend Andrew Johns who at the time was the NSW assistant coach caused controversy when it emerged that he had encouraged a player to attack Queensland player Greg Inglis, referring to him as a "black cunt". Gidley defended Andrew Johns as not a racist and stipulated that "He's obviously still going to be a great mate of mine."<ref name="NSW skipper Kurt Gidley says Johns not a racist">{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nsw-skipper-kurt-gidley-says-johns-not-a-racist/story-e6frf9if-1225880062939|title=NSW skipper Kurt Gidley says Johns not a racist|publisher=heraldsun.com.au|date=15 June 2010|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref> Game 3 of the [[2010 State of Origin series]] saw Gidley again starting from the bench, and this time stripped of his captaincy. Gidley came on to replace [[Michael Ennis]] after 30 minutes and went on to play the rest of the game. Just before half-time, Gidley set up an inside pass for {{rlp|sr}} [[Paul Gallen]] to crash over the try line for New South Wales first try of the game. In the 47th minute, Kurt scored the next try for the Blues which narrowed the point deficit to just one. He continued to make several linebreaks and defended strong. Gidley was later awarded the [[Brad Fittler Medal]] for NSW's Player of the Series.<ref name="Bellamy Takes Positives">{{cite web|url=http://bunbury.igwn.com.au/index.php/sport/league/bellamy-takes-positives,21522441|title=Bellamy Takes Positives|publisher=bunbury.igwn.com.au|date=6 February 2016|access-date=1 January 2017}}{{dead link|date=May 2020|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> At the end of the 2010 season, Gidley was selected to play for Australia for the [[Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations Tournament]]. He played in 3 out of the 4 games and played in the final against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. He played the whole 2nd half of the final in the {{rlp|ce}}s due to a knee injury to teammate [[Brent Tate]].{{cn|date=April 2024}}

2011 saw Gidley selected in the [[NRL All Stars team]] for a second year in a row after [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] used one of his two personal picks for Gidley.{{cn|date=June 2025}}

Gidley was selected for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[2013 ANZAC Test]] and played from the interchange bench. In what was the first [[Test match (rugby league)|test match]] ever played in [[Canberra]], [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] were defeated.

On 5 May 2015, Gidley signed a 1-year contract with [[Super League]] team [[Warrington Wolves]], with an option for a second year, starting in 2016, ending a 15-year stint in the [[National Rugby League]].<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/05/05/gidley_decides_on_fu.html|title=Gidley decides on future - Knights|date=5 May 2015 |publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2016-02-06}}</ref>

On his Super League début for the Wolves, Gidley converted 2 goals in a man of the match performance against 2015 champions the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. In April, he picked up the Super League Player of the Month award.<ref name="Gidley named First Utility Super League Player of the Month">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugby-league.com/article/37500/gidley-named-first-utility-super-league-player-of-the-month|title=Gidley named First Utility Super League Player of the Month|publisher=rugby-league.com|date=11 June 2016|access-date=1 January 2017}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

In June 2017 it was announced that Gidley would retire at the end of the 2017 season.<ref name="Kurt Gidley: Warrington Wolves half-back to retire at end of 2017 season">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40432055|title=Kurt Gidley: Warrington Wolves half-back to retire at end of 2017 season|work=bbc.co.uk|date=28 June 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Gidley is founder and co-owner of Steel City Beer Company with [[Andrew Johns]], [[Matthew Johns]], [[Danny Buderus]], and [[Matt Hoy]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://pubtic.com.au/newcastle-legends-behind-launch-of-steel-city-beer-co/#:~:text=Steel%20City%27s%20founders%20include%20proud,and%20Sophie%20and%20Ryan%20Ginns. | title=Newcastle Legends Behind Launch of Steel City Beer Co | date=16 April 2021 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/7663201/knights-say-cheers-to-stadium-beers/ | title=Knights say cheers to stadium beers | date=18 March 2022 }}</ref>
